title: Ultimate Easy to Build Craft Table
summary: A butcher-block-topped craft table on two shelf towers — one tower of open 1x8 cubbies, one of deep 1x12 shelves, with two drawers built to fit the gap between them.
description:
This table is really two different storage towers sharing one long butcher-block top: a shallow 1x8 cubby tower for supplies you want to see and grab, and a deep 1x12 shelf tower for the bulkier stuff, joined by a pair of drawers in the middle. Both towers use the same joinery — pocket holes and pocket hole screws are what the plan recommends, though 2" self-tapping screws and glue work as a substitute; brad nails alone are specifically called out as not strong enough to hold up over time here.

The 1x4 board inset at the top back of each 1x12 shelf isn't decorative — it adds real structure to the shelf assembly and gives the drawer slides something solid to screw into later. Build the shelf sides and this back support before anything else, since everything downstream references it.

The drawers are sized to fit the gap left between the two towers once they're both screwed to the underside of the countertop with the correct 24-1/2" spacing between them — build the towers and set that spacing first, then measure your own actual opening before cutting drawer parts, since the drawer cut list is explicitly approximate. If you'd rather have one continuous shelf instead of a cubby tower up front, the plan allows a straightforward substitution: extend the 1x12 shelves to run the full 28-1/2" length and skip the 1x8 front shelf entirely.

materials.name: Drawer slides, 12" side mount full extension

cutList.note: Measure your actual opening before cutting — approximate.

steps.title: Build the shelf tower sides
steps.body: Build the 1x12 side shelves with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, or 2" self-tapping screws with glue if you'd rather skip the jig — brad nails alone aren't recommended for this joint. Add the 1x4 board inset at the top back of each shelf; it braces the assembly and gives you something solid to screw a drawer slide into later.

steps.title: Build and attach the cubby tower
steps.body: Build the 1x8 front shelves and attach them to the front of each 1x12 shelf tower with screws. This is a good time to paint or finish the shelves before the top goes on.

steps.title: Attach the towers to the top
steps.body: Carefully attach both shelf towers to the underside of the butcher block top, leaving a 24-1/2" gap between them so the drawers will fit. Attach with screws through the top shelf.

steps.title: Build and fit the drawers
steps.body: Measure the actual 24-1/2" gap between the towers before cutting drawer parts — the drawer cut list is approximate. Build each drawer box from the 1x3 sides, front, and back with pocket holes and glue, add the plywood bottom, mount on the 12" drawer slides, and attach a 1x4 drawer face centered on the opening.
materials: Drawer slides, 12" side mount full extension

steps.title: Sand and finish
steps.body: Sand the whole table, touch up any exposed edges, and finish to match your space.

images.alt: Craft table with two storage towers and a butcher block top
